prompt:
The community-garden plot roster tool stopped building after we standardized the repo on node's native TypeScript running (node 26 type stripping, nodenext resolution, verbatim module syntax — tsconfig is already set up and is not up for debate). Someone did half the migration and went on leave. `bash run_verify.sh` is the CI gate (npm ci first; it type-checks, then runs the suite under node --test). Current wreckage:

  assign.ts(1,10): error TS1484: 'Plot' is a type and must be imported using a type-only import when 'verbatimModuleSyntax' is enabled.
  fees.ts(1,24): error TS2835: Relative import paths need explicit file extensions in ECMAScript imports when '--moduleResolution' is 'node16' or 'nodenext'. Did you mean './plot.js'?
  parse.ts(1,27): error TS2835: Relative import paths need explicit file extensions in ECMAScript imports when '--moduleResolution' is 'node16' or 'nodenext'. Did you mean './plot.js'?
  roster.ts(1,8): error TS2613: Module '"parse"' has no default export. Did you mean to use 'import { parseRoster } from "parse"' instead?
  roster.ts(33,15): error TS1361: 'formatFee' cannot be used as a value because it was imported using 'import type'.

Heads up from the last person who tried: don't just paste in what the compiler suggests without thinking — the second gate actually EXECUTES these files with node, so whatever you import has to exist on disk at runtime too. Zone rules, fees, and seating order are all pinned by test_plotroster.ts (read-only, as are run_verify.sh, tsconfig.json and the package manifests). Small fixes only, no restructuring.
notes: Module-resolution triage under nodenext + verbatimModuleSyntax + allowImportingTsExtensions (typescript 5.9.3 pinned devDependency, npm ci via reference_setup): two relative imports missing their explicit ./x.ts extension (parse.ts, fees.ts — TS2835; the compiler's own suggestion './plot.js' type-checks but then dies at the node --test runtime gate because no .js file exists, which is the trap the prompt warns about), a type imported as a value import under verbatimModuleSyntax (assign.ts — TS1484), a value used from an 'import type' (roster.ts formatFee — TS1361), and a default import of a named-only export (roster.ts parseRoster — TS2613). All 5 diagnostics surface in one tsc pass; node --test (type stripping) is the second gate and independently fails on every one of these at baseline. Absolute workspace paths in the real TS2613 message were shortened in the prompt paste. Domain note: go-planttags is a plant-tag registry; this is garden-plot seating/fees glue — different layer and API surface, overlap is name-deep only. Toolchain observed: node 26.3.0, tsc 5.9.3.
